subtitle: A peninsula sealed off from its own mainland by Permian limestone, reachable only by boat, that became one of the world's capitals of tropical rock climbing.
spike_statement: A peninsula no road reaches: limestone walls 245 million years old wall it off from the mainland, so the only way in is a longtail boat.

levers.photo_tone: jade Andaman water against grey-gold Ratburi limestone, stalactite shadow under overhanging walls
